updates.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
updates.summary: Anthropic launched India-specific rupee pricing for all Claude subscription tiers on July 13, 2026, targeting its second-largest global market where it accounts for 5.8% of worldwide Claude usage. Claude Pro is listed at ₹2,000/month (annual, ~$21) vs. $17 in the US; Claude Max starts at ₹11,999/month (~$125) vs. $100 in the US; and Team plans begin at ₹2,399/seat/month (~$25) vs. $20 in the US. UPI — India's dominant payment rail — is not yet supported; users must pay by card or through Apple/Google app store billing. The move follows Anthropic opening a Bengaluru office in February 2026, appointing former Microsoft India MD Irina Ghose to lead its India operations, and signing enterprise partnerships with Infosys and TCS.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed across TechCrunch, Business Standard, Eastern Herald, Analytics Insight, and CryptoBriefing — all citing the same price points. UPI limitation corroborated by multiple independent reports. Anthropic's Bengaluru office and Irina Ghose appointment are verifiable facts. Pricing comparisons to US rates are consistent across all sources. No red flags.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.208: Screen Reader Mode, Vim Remaps, Corporate Launcher Support
updates.summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Code v2.1.208 on July 14, 2026, with 45 changes. The headline feature is opt-in screen reader mode — activate via `claude --ax-screen-reader`, the environment variable `CLAUDE_AX_SCREEN_READER=1`, or `"axScreenReader": true` in settings — which renders plain text optimized for NVDA and JAWS, hides decorative glyphs, and labels transcript symbols as short readable strings. Other additions include a `vimInsertModeRemaps` setting for mapping two-key sequences (e.g. `jj` → Escape) in vim mode, `CLAUDE_CODE_PROCESS_WRAPPER` to force every Claude Code self-spawn through a corporate launcher executable (enterprise compliance use case), and mouse-click support for multi-select menus in fullscreen mode. Bug fixes address fast mode failing to restore when switching back to a supported model, and background-agent replies being silently dropped on delivery failure.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced from official Anthropic Claude Code release notes via multiple changelog trackers (Releasebot, GitHub anthropics/claude-code releases, and developer changelog sites). The specific version number (2.1.208) and feature list are consistent across all trackers. No red flags.

updates.title: Claude Platform API Updates: Key Expiration, Rate Limit Parity, Opus 4.7 Fast Mode Deprecation
updates.summary: Anthropic published a batch of Claude Platform API updates on July 14, 2026 covering security, billing, rate limits, and model lifecycle. API key expiration is now configurable in the Claude Console (preset, custom, or never) with email notifications before expiry for keys with at least 7-day lifetimes. Rate limits for Claude Sonnet and Claude Haiku have been raised to match Claude Opus across all three usage tiers (Start, Build, Scale). Fast mode for Claude Opus 4.7 is deprecated effective today, with hard removal on July 24, 2026 — after which requests with `speed: "fast"` on claude-opus-4-7 will return an error; Anthropic recommends migrating to fast mode on Claude Opus 4.8. A new `agent-memory-2026-07-22` beta header changes memory-listing behavior (stable server-defined order, stricter depth/path_prefix validation). Billing improvement: requests that return `stop_reason: "refusal"` with zero generated tokens are no longer charged. All major SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, Ruby, PHP, C#) now include code execution tool support for `code_execution_20260120`, enabling REPL state persistence and programmatic tool calling.
updates.accuracy_notes: Release notes content confirmed via Releasebot, releases.sh, and multiple developer changelog trackers that mirror docs.anthropic.com. The July 24 removal date for Opus 4.7 fast mode is specific enough to be verifiable. Billing change (no charge for refusals) is a notable policy shift worth flagging as high-value for API users. No red flags.
